expectations.text: The output searches or reasons about both Inbox and Sent Items, not just Inbox
expectations.evidence: Header: 'Found 17 PLM-bound emails (14 received, 3 sent)'; includes 📤-marked sent items (e.g. Alvin's outbound F&A approval to KDC-One, PO #100310 deposit-invoice reply).

expectations.text: Emails are classified into named flows (A through G, or I) rather than left unclassified
expectations.evidence: Explicit section headers 'PURCHASE ORDERS (Flow A)', 'FORMULA / ARTWORK APPROVALS (Flow D)', 'COMPONENT / BOM UPDATES (Flow G)', 'VENDOR UPDATES (Flow C)', 'VENDOR INVOICES — COST LEDGER (Flow I)', with each line item tagged INSERT/UPDATE.

expectations.text: The non-PLM signal scan runs alongside the PLM classification
expectations.evidence: Dedicated QUALITY CROSS-FLAGS, REGULATORY CROSS-FLAGS, MARGIN CROSS-FLAGS, and OUTLOOK-ASANA-BRIDGE CROSS-FLAGS sections are present and explain their empty/non-empty state (e.g. TikTok ad spend flagged as non-PLM signal).

expectations.text: The output uses the PLM Triage Report section structure
expectations.evidence: Matches the skill's format closely: 🚨 URGENT, 📋 PURCHASE ORDERS, 📋 FORMULA APPROVALS, 📋 COMPONENT/BOM, 📋 VENDOR UPDATES, 📋 VENDOR INVOICES, cross-flag sections, ending in the 'Which should I push?' prompt.

expectations.text: No PLM INSERT/UPDATE is claimed as already committed
expectations.evidence: Closing line: 'Nothing was written to PLM, Asana, or anywhere else in this pass — this is the triage report only.'

claims.claim: Several Ramp-sourced payees (Studebaker Brackett PLLC, Erin Entrup, LACY REDWAY INC., WILHELMINA INTERNATIONAL INC.) don't match any PLM vendor/partner/contact
claims.evidence: Run correctly applied the 'surface unrecognized entities rather than drop them' rule from the skill's run-time entity discovery section instead of silently skipping or guessing.

eval_feedback.overall: Full pass with substantive evidence. The run also self-reported its own coverage gaps (vendors with no contact_email on file, an unrecognized OC3PL folder name) rather than silently under-searching — a good sign the skill's instructions are being followed in spirit, not just pattern-matched.
